docs.title: 📘 Project Purpose
docs.text:
The Domain Glossary System is designed to support teams in building a shared understanding of a project's domain language. It helps document and manage the ubiquitous language—the core terminology used in domain-driven design (DDD)—ensuring alignment between domain experts and technical teams.

By organizing the domain into Projects → Contexts → Terms, this system acts as a single source of truth for:

  • Standardizing definitions used across teams.
  • Capturing examples and usage scenarios from domain conversations.
  • Reducing ambiguity in communication between developers and domain experts.
  • Enabling smoother translation of domain concepts into software models and code.

docs.title: 1. Project
docs.text: Represents the overall business or technical initiative (e.g., "E-commerce Platform", "HRMS System").

docs.title: 2. Context
docs.text: Represents a bounded area within a project where specific language is used consistently (e.g., "Checkout Context", "Leave Management Context").

docs.title: 3. Term
docs.text: Each term includes: - Name: The keyword or concept (e.g., "Cart", "Leave Request"). - Definition: A clear, concise explanation of the term. - Examples: Real-world usage or domain conversation quotes that illustrate the term in context.

docs.title: 🎯 Goal
docs.text:
The goal of this system is to bridge the gap between business and technical understanding by maintaining a living domain dictionary that evolves alongside the project. This makes it easier to:

  • Convert domain knowledge into domain models.
  • Write meaningful and context-aware code.
  • Support training, onboarding, and documentation with domain-rich content.

docs.title: 🛠️ Use Cases
docs.text:
• Capture terms discussed during domain discovery and modeling sessions.
  • Improve communication between developers, analysts, and domain experts.
  • Drive consistency in code by aligning it with domain definitions.
  • Prevent misunderstandings and duplication of logic due to vague terminology.

“A shared language between business and developers is the foundation of good software.” — Inspired by Domain-Driven Design principles

docs.title: Domain Glossary System Backend Container Diagram
How to View the ER Diagram
docs.text:
The Mermaid erDiagram syntax may not render properly on this site due to limitations of the current Mermaid version.

To view the ER diagram correctly, follow these steps:

docs.title: 📤 Steps to View in Mermaid Live Editor
docs.text:
1. Open the Mermaid Live Editor.
  2. Copy the content below and paste it into the left-hand editor panel.
  3. Click the "Full screen" button in the top-right to expand the diagram.

docs.title: 📋 Diagram Code (copy this)
docs.text:
C4Container
    title Backend Container diagram for Domain Glossary System

    Container_Boundary(c1, "Domain Glossary System") {
        Container(spa, "Single Page App", "Angular", "Provides all the domain glossary management <br/> functionality to users via <br/>their web browser")
        ContainerDb(dgs_db, "Database", "MySQL Database", "Stores dgs related data to database")

        Container_Boundary(c2, "Backend") {
            Container(dgs_gateway, "API Gateway", "Java, Spring Boot", "Gateway for backend <br/> all Rest APIs")

            Container_Boundary(c3, "Backend Private Network") {
                Container(dgs_api, "API Container", "Java, Spring Boot", "Ingestion service")
                Container(dgs_auth, "Auth Container", "Java, Spring Boot", "Auth service")
                Container(dgs_batch, "Batch Container", "Java, Spring Boot", "Batch service")
                Container(dgs_notificaton, "Notification Container", "Java, Spring Boot, <br/> Java Mail APi", "Notification service")
            }
        }
    }

     Rel(spa, dgs_gateway, "Uses", "HTTPS")

     Rel(dgs_gateway, dgs_api, "Uses", "HTTP")
     Rel(dgs_gateway, dgs_auth, "Uses", "HTTP")
     Rel(dgs_gateway, dgs_batch, "Uses", "HTTP")
     Rel(dgs_gateway, dgs_notificaton, "Uses", "HTTP")

     Rel(dgs_api, dgs_db, "Uses", "JPA")
     Rel(dgs_auth, dgs_db, "Uses", "JPA")
     Rel(dgs_batch, dgs_db, "Uses", "JPA")
     Rel(dgs_notificaton, dgs_db, "Uses", "JPA")


    UpdateLayoutConfig($c4ShapeInRow="4", $c4BoundaryInRow="1")

docs.title: How to View the ER Diagram
docs.text:
The Mermaid erDiagram syntax may not render properly on this site due to limitations of the current Mermaid version.

To view the ER diagram correctly, follow these steps:

docs.title: 📤 Steps to View in Mermaid Live Editor
docs.text:
1. Open the Mermaid Live Editor.
  2. Copy the content below and paste it into the left-hand editor panel.
  3. Click the "Full screen" button in the top-right to expand the diagram.

docs.title: 📋 Diagram Code (copy this)
docs.text:
C4Container
    title Container diagram for Domain Glossary System

    System_Ext(google_auth_server, "Google Auth Server", "Responsible to authenticate users <br/> using sign with google")
    Person(admin, "Admin", "A user who is going to resposnible <br/> to manage organizations, <br/> users and other everything")
    Person(managers, "Managers", "A user who is going to resposnible <br/> to manage projects, <br/> contexts,terms and project assignment")        
    Person(members, "Members", "A user who is belongs to <br/> one or many projects <br/> and know project doamin using context and terms")
    System_Ext(fcs_blob_storage, "Firebase Clound Storage", "Responsible to store binary files <br/> like images, pdf, videos, audios etc..")


    Container_Boundary(c1, "Domain Glossary System") {
        Container(spa, "Single Page App", "Angular", "Provides all the domain glossary management <br/> functionality to users via <br/>their web browser")
        Container(dgs_backend_system, "Backend", "Java, Spring Boot", "Microservice aarchitecture based backend")
        ContainerDb(dgs_db, "Database", "MySQL Database", "Stores dgs related data to database")
    }

    Rel(admin, spa, "Uses", "HTTPS")
    Rel(managers, spa, "Uses", "HTTPS")
    Rel(members, spa, "Uses", "HTTPS")

    Rel(spa, google_auth_server, "Uses")
    Rel(spa, fcs_blob_storage, "Uses")

    Rel(dgs_backend_system, google_auth_server, "Uses")
    Rel(dgs_backend_system, fcs_blob_storage, "Uses")

    BiRel(spa, dgs_backend_system, "Uses")
    BiRel(dgs_backend_system, dgs_db, "Uses")


    UpdateLayoutConfig($c4ShapeInRow="3", $c4BoundaryInRow="1")

docs.title: TDD 1  Setup new API container for DGS
TDD 1 : Setup new API container for DGS
docs.text: Github Issue: GH-4

docs.title: Problem Context
docs.text: We need to create new spring boot application which will act as API container i.e it will have our all functionalities of DGS system.

docs.title: Steps to create spring boot application for DGS API container
docs.text:
• Go to Spring Initializr
  • Select Maven
  • Select latest stable spring boot version (3.5.3)
  • Fill project metadata like below
  • Group: com.amalvadkar
  • Artifact: dgs-service-api
  • Name: dgs-service-api
  • Description: DGS API Container
  • Package name: com.amalvadkar.dgs
  • Packaging: Jar
  • Java: Select latest LTS version (21)
  • Select below dependencies
  • Spring Web
  • Spring Data JPA
  • MySQL Driver
  • Liquibase Migration
  • Lombok
  • Validation
  • Spring Boot DevTools
  • Spring Configuration Processor
  • Spring Boot Actuator
  • Testcontainers
  • Generate and unzip
  • Open in IntelliJ
  • Create git repository
  • Share project on GitHub in public mode
  • Mark GitHub ticket GH-5 as DONE

docs.title: TDD-2 : Setup Predefined Users and Roles
Problem Context
docs.text:
In DGS system we will have some predefined users and roles like below

Predefined users: * System

Predefined roles: * Admin * Responsible to manager everything along with users and organizations * Manager * Responsible to manager projects, contexts and terms along with user assignment to project * Member * Responsible to view terms and contexts related to assigned projects * System * Responsible to send email notification and other automated tasks

We need to setup these users and roles in DGS database and also provide required support in DGS API Container.

Need to write tests as well to check that implemented solution is working as expected or not ?

docs.title: users Table
docs.text: Column Data Type Constraints id VARCHAR(45) Primary Key name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL email VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, UNIQUE last_login_time DATETIME Nullable active BIT(1) NOT NULL, DEFAULT 1 role_id VARCHAR(45) NOT NULL, Foreign Key → roles.id created_by VARCHAR(45) Nullable, Foreign Key → users.id created_on DATETIME Nullable updated_by VARCHAR(45) Nullable, Foreign Key → users.id updated_on DATETIME Nullable delete_flag BIT(1) DEFAULT 0

docs.title: roles Table
docs.text: Column Data Type Constraints id VARCHAR(45) Primary Key name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, UNIQUE description VARCHAR(255) Nullable

docs.title: Create below INSERT Statements
docs.text:
• Insert Admin, Manager, System and Member roles into roles table (Check Predefined roles section for description)
  • Insert System user with System role in users table
  • Use ULID Generator to generate ULID for above INSERT statements
  • Keep them in liquibase section of dgs-service-api container

docs.title: Service: dgs-service-api
docs.text:
• Create required JPA entities (RoleEntity, UserEntity)
  • Create BaseEntity to re-use common JPA entity properties
  • Create required Spring Data JPA repositories (RoleRepo, UserRepo)
  • Write DataJpaTest (Slice Test) to check inserted predefined users and roles are inserted properly in DGS database or not

docs.title: Final Thoughts
docs.text: Learn during implementation, don't implement for the sac of implementation only
